data.title: Bitcoin Attracts Smart Money, Says F2Pool Co-Founder
data.description: On February 14, F2Pool co-founder Wang Chun shared on social media that smart money is currently investing in Bitcoin. According to BlockBeats, this statement highlights a growing interest in Bitcoin among informed investors. Wang Chun's comments suggest a positive sentiment towards the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Solana Advisor Announces API Policy Update to Prevent Unauthorized Fee Pools
data.description: On February 14, Nikita Bier, the head of product for X and an advisor to Solana, announced plans to update the API policy. According to BlockBeats, the update aims to prevent applications from creating fee pools without user consent. Bier emphasized the importance of user agreement in the development of such features, highlighting the company's commitment to transparency and user rights.

data.title: Knife Attack at Paris Landmark Ends with Suspect Shot by Police
data.description: A man on a watchlist was shot dead by police after injuring a person with a knife near the Arc de Triomphe in Paris on Friday, the 13th. According to Ming Pao, French President Emmanuel Macron praised the police for successfully preventing a potential terrorist attack.

data.title: Product Manager X: Proposed API policy update to block unauthorized applications creating fee pools.
data.description: Nikita Bier, product lead for X, stated on the X platform that she considers spam emails offering "fee collection" to be extremely dishonest. Bier pointed out that once a developer engages in such behavior, every reply they receive for the rest of their career will be bombarded with requests to raise prices. Bier explicitly stated that she intends to update API policies to ban applications that create fee pools without user consent.

data.title: Cocoa Market Fluctuations Impact West African Trade Practices
data.description:
Cocoa prices have experienced significant volatility, affecting traditional business operations in West Africa, the primary supplier of cocoa beans globally. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the dramatic rise and subsequent fall in cocoa prices, which are prompting changes in the region's trade dynamics. The fluctuations are challenging established practices and may lead to adjustments in how cocoa is marketed and sold.

West Africa, known for its substantial contribution to the global cocoa supply, is facing pressure to adapt to these market changes. The region's producers and traders are reevaluating strategies to cope with the unpredictable price movements. This shift could influence the global cocoa market, as West African countries play a crucial role in meeting international demand.

The recent price instability underscores the need for more resilient business models and strategies to mitigate risks associated with such volatility. As the market continues to evolve, stakeholders in West Africa are exploring new approaches to ensure sustainability and profitability in the cocoa industry.

data.title: “Everyone Is Short” As Crypto Flashes Bear-Market Exit Signs
data.description:
Despite brutal selling and some of the most extreme readings in Bitcoin, crypto's laying the groundwork for a bear‑market exit.
source: https://dailycoin.com/everyone-is-short-as-crypto-flashes-bear-market-exit-signs/?utm_source=cryptopanic&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Bitcoin Seems to Reflects Institutional Stress as ETF Pressure Builds
data.description:
The projected $646 billion AI spending pipeline for 2026 is colliding with weakening balance-sheet flexibility across major technology firms. That capital intensity shift is altering how institutional capital interprets both Big Tech and Bitcoin. According to analysis shared by CryptoQuant, Bitcoin is no longer behaving as a decorrelated hedge
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-seems-to-reflects-institutional-stress-as-etf-pressure-builds/

data.title: Anthropic user base surges 11% after Super Bowl ad bashing OpenAI goes viral
data.description:
Anthropic’s user base increased by 11% after its viral Super Bowl ad, which bashed rival OpenAI and earned it bragging rights, according to BNP Paribas.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/anthropic-user-base-surges-11-openai/

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. Secretary of State Rubio Addresses Transatlantic Era Concerns
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has clarified the United States' stance amidst discussions about the end of the transatlantic era. According to Jin10, Rubio emphasized that the notion of concluding the transatlantic era is neither a goal nor a desire of the United States. His statement comes at a time when news surrounding the potential end of this era is prevalent, underscoring the U.S.'s commitment to maintaining strong transatlantic relations.

data.title: A whale that had accumulated long positions of 105,000 ETH has turned a profit, now holding a floating profit of $2.167 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a whale that had accumulated a long position of 105,000 ETH has recovered over $10 million in unrealized losses and is currently showing a profit of $2.167 million. Specifically, address 0xa5B…01D41 holds 60,000 ETH, worth $123 million, with an opening price of $2048.9, resulting in a profit of $749,000; address 0x6C8…D84F6 holds 45,000 ETH, worth $92.82 million, with an opening price of $2029.38, resulting in a profit of $1.418 million.

data.title: Cambodia's De-Dollarization Effort Aims to Boost Local Currency Use
data.description: Cambodia is taking steps to promote the use of its local currency, the riel, by implementing a small-scale de-dollarization initiative. According to NS3.AI, the country plans to phase out smaller US dollar bills, specifically $1, $2, and $5 denominations, to encourage merchants and consumers to use the riel for everyday transactions. As part of this initiative, ATMs will now dispense only $100 bills, making small-dollar transactions less practical and steering daily currency circulation towards the riel. This move is intended to increase the use of the riel in daily transactions while reserving US dollars for larger payments.

data.title: Ukraine to Discuss New IMF Program Without Immediate Preconditions
data.description: Ukraine announced that discussions regarding its new program with the International Monetary Fund (IMF) will take place in the coming weeks. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these talks will proceed without any immediate preconditions, such as the tax changes Kyiv was expected to implement this month. The decision marks a significant step in Ukraine's ongoing financial negotiations with the IMF, as it seeks to secure further economic support without the need for immediate policy adjustments.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. Secretary of State Rubio Emphasizes Ally Strength
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio stated that the United States does not wish for its allies to become weak. According to Jin10, Rubio emphasized the importance of self-defense among allies to deter any adversaries from testing the limits of U.S. commitments. This stance highlights the strategic focus on strengthening alliances to maintain global stability and security.

data.title: Stablecoin Liquidity Builds on Exchanges as Bitcoin Spot Demand Fails to Expand
data.description:
The divergence between exchange-held stablecoins and Bitcoin’s weakening spot structure becomes visible once flows since April 7, 2025 are mapped together. Liquidity has not exited the ecosystem, yet it has not meaningfully absorbed BTC supply either.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/stablecoin-liquidity-builds-on-exchanges-as-bitcoin-spot-demand-fails-to-expand/

data.title: Bank’s Anti-Fraud Agent Steals Customers’ Identities, Drains $2,000,000 From Victims’ Accounts
data.description:
A US bank employee hired to protect people from fraud ended up stealing $2 million from customers, according to the U. S. Department of Justice
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2026/02/14/banks-anti-fraud-agent-steals-customers-identities-drains-2000000-from-victims-accounts/

data.title: Central Bank of Russia Signals Policy Shift With 2026 Stablecoin Feasibility Study
data.description:
On February 13, 2026, First Deputy Governor Vladimir Chistyukhin confirmed that the regulator will conduct a feasibility study in 2026 before presenting findings for public discussion. The decision to formally examine a national stablecoin marks a notable recalibration in the Central Bank of Russia’s digital asset strategy.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/central-bank-of-russia-signals-policy-shift-with-2026-stablecoin-feasibility-study/

data.title: Solana Co-Founder Anatoly Yakovenko's Wealth Tied to SOL Holdings
data.description:
An analysis report by Arkham has estimated the net worth of Solana co-founder Anatoly Yakovenko to be between $500 million and $1.2 billion. According to PANews, this wealth primarily stems from his holdings in SOL tokens and equity in Solana Labs, closely linked to the market price of Solana.

At Solana's inception, 500 million SOL tokens were minted, with 12.5% allocated to the founding team. It is widely speculated that the address 9QgXq is associated with Yakovenko, holding over 136,000 SOL. Between August and November 2024, this account unstaked and transferred over 3 million SOL, with more than 1.5 million restaked to a new address. If these addresses belong to Yakovenko, his SOL holdings are valued at approximately $122 million. Additionally, a domain associated with his username "Toly" holds about $16,500 in liquid assets.

Yakovenko owns approximately 5-10% equity in Solana Labs, which has received investments from firms like a16z, Polychain Capital, and Multicoin Capital. The company's valuation is estimated between $5 billion and $8 billion, making his equity worth around $250 million to $800 million. Furthermore, he has invested as an angel investor in over 40 projects within the Solana ecosystem, including Jito Labs, Drift Protocol, and Helius.

data.title: CEO Sentenced to 20 Years for $200 Million Bitcoin Ponzi Scheme
data.description: The CEO of Praetorian Group International has been sentenced to 20 years in prison for orchestrating a $200 million Bitcoin Ponzi scheme. According to NS3.AI, the fraudulent activities occurred between December 2019 and October 2021, affecting over 90,000 investors worldwide. This case underscores the persistent risks linked to cryptocurrency investment scams.

data.title: XT is about to launch BREM
data.description: XT.COM will soon list BREM and open the BREM/USDT trading pair in the Innovation Zone (Layer 1). The specific listing date will be announced separately. BREM is a blockchain-based utility token focused on building a multi-vendor voucher and membership benefits exchange market, connecting consumers and merchants across the ecosystem. Users can manage and redeem digital vouchers, gift cards, and membership benefits across different brands using a single wallet and token, enabling cross-platform value circulation.
